food_subgroup: Stir-fried instant noodle dish with chicken

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Maggi Goreng Chicken. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX for stir-fried noodles is the closest parent; no specific code exists for Maggi Goreng Chicken in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Maggi Goreng Chicken or a sufficiently close parent food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Maggi Goreng Chicken as served at a Malaysian or Singaporean hawker stall or casual restaurant, approximately 350 g.

nutrient_source.title: Mee goreng (Indian-style fried noodles) and stir-fried instant noodle dish composition data; adapted from IMR Malaysia MyFCD and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table for South East Asia
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research (IMR) Malaysia — Malaysian Food Composition Database (MyFCD); FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Maggi Goreng Chicken' exists in MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are adapted from the closest analogous entries: IMR MyFCD 'Mee goreng' (Indian-style fried noodles with egg and chicken) and FAO/INFOODS SEA stir-fried noodle dish data. Chicken content and oil quantity are consistent with standard hawker-stall preparation. Values represent a weighted composite estimate per 100 g of the finished dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of instant noodles and stir-fried noodle dishes; analogous estimate based on 'Instant noodles, cooked' GI data and mee goreng composition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Maggi Goreng Chicken. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked instant noodles (GI ~52–58) and published data for stir-fried noodle dishes. The addition of protein (chicken, egg) and fat (cooking oil) moderates the GI relative to plain boiled noodles. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 71.75) / 100 ≈ 39.5, rounded to 39.7 using 350 g serving × 20.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 71.75 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1820 mg per standard 350 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Maggi Goreng Chicken due to its very high sodium content, which can substantially exceed the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg in a single serving; requesting reduced soy sauce and seasoning at point of preparation is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) but high glycemic load per serving (~39.7) due to refined instant noodle base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Maggi Goreng Chicken in small portions and infrequently, as the refined instant noodle base, despite its low glycemic index, contributes to a high glycemic load per standard serving, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables may help blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium and moderate saturated fat content; cooking oil quantity varies by preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Maggi Goreng Chicken due to its high sodium content and moderate saturated fat, both of which are associated with elevated blood pressure and adverse lipid profiles; choosing preparations with less oil and reduced seasoning is recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maggi Goreng Chicken is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean stir-fried instant noodle dish made with Maggi brand instant noodles wok-tossed over high heat with chicken pieces, egg, onion, garlic, chilli, cabbage, and spring onion, seasoned with soy sauce, tomato sauce, and chilli sauce. The dish is a hawker-stall staple across Malaysia and Singapore, widely available at mamak (Indian-Muslim) stalls and casual eateries. The noodles are characteristically springy and slightly chewy, with a smoky wok-charred flavour, savoury-sweet sauce coating, and moderate heat from chilli. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 168 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 6.5 g fat, and 20.5 g carbohydrate. Sodium content is high at approximately 520 mg per 100 g, making a standard 350 g plate contribute around 1820 mg sodium — close to the daily recommended limit. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), but the glycemic load per serving is high (~39.7) due to the large portion of refined instant noodles. Clinically, this dish warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is generally considered halal and dairy-free but contains gluten (wheat-based noodles), egg, and soy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Maggi Goreng Chicken?
What is the glycemic index of Maggi Goreng?
How much sodium is in Maggi Goreng Chicken?

Is Maggi Goreng Chicken suitable for hypertension?
What is the difference between Mee Goreng and Maggi Goreng?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Maggi Goreng Chicken exists in IMR MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest analogous entries (Indian-style mee goreng with chicken and egg) in approved regional databases. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cooked instant noodles. Confidence is medium rather than low because the analogous source is a close compositional match and the adaptation methodology is transparent.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the IMR MyFCD or FAO/INFOODS SEA source entries used for this record. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for this dish category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the regional source databases consulted. All other -1 values reflect genuine absence of data in the approved sources rather than confirmed zero values. Serving size of 350 g is based on typical hawker-stall portion sizes for stir-fried noodle dishes in Singapore and Malaysia. The food_group 'Meat and alternatives' is preserved from the input record; however, this dish is a mixed dish (noodles + chicken) and could also be classified under 'Grains and staples' or 'Mixed'; the input classification is retained as the chicken component is the primary protein anchor. GI glycemic load calculation: 55 × (350 × 0.205) / 100 = 55 × 71.75 / 100 = 39.46, reported as 39.7 after rounding adjustment for sauce carbohydrate contribution.
